summary: base62-86x exfiltrates decode() input to attacker-controlled Telegram bots
details: The package impersonates the legitimate base-x library (spoofed author, homepage, and copied source) and modifies its documented decode(string) API to silently transmit the caller-supplied input to attacker-controlled Telegram bots. In src/cjs/index.cjs (lines 113-114), an obfuscated string-array routine constructs https://api.telegram.org/bot<redacted>/sendMessage and POSTs a JSON body of {chat_id: 7974622428, text: <caller input>} via fetch, wrapped in try/catch so failures are invisible. The src/esm/index.js variant embeds the same behavior inside a ~130KB custom VM bytecode interpreter that decodes a base64 blob containing a second Telegram bot token and chat_id 7959381237; the VM additionally includes debugger-trap opcodes and timing-based anti-analysis. base-x decode is commonly used on base58/62 wallet addresses, keys, and other secret material, so the advertised API itself functions as a data-exfiltration channel. Package name base62-86x is a typosquat of base-x with a divergent malicious API; author/homepage metadata is spoofed to match the legitimate cryptocoinjs/base-x project.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
